name: Booze Beans Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 4-6 peppercorns (or lots of fresh ground pepper)
3 cans favorite beans, drained and rinsed (or 6 cups cooked beans)
enough wine to make it soupy (for every 3 wine, add 1 water)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, saute the chopped onion in red wine until soft.
2. Add mushrooms and continue sauteing until liquid begins to evaporate.
3. Stir in garlic, bay leaf, peppercorns, thyme, salt, and beans.
4. Pour in enough wine to make it soupy (for every 3 wine, add 1 water).
5. Simmer for a long time and let sit overnight.
Second night:
6. Bring the pot to a boil, stir in frozen peas, and top with dumplings.
7. Mix flour, egg replacer, milk, baking powder, salt, pepper, and rosemary into a dough.
8. Drop spoonfuls of dough into the boiling pot.
9. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes (do not turn down heat!).
Both recipes adapted from The Joy of Cooking.
